Name: Amaya
Age & grade: 13, 7th grade.

Interests & passions:
Serious ballet dancer — six days a week at the local company's pre-pro
program, currently working on Vaganova syllabus level 4. She thinks in terms
of port-de-bras lines and counts musicality in fractions. She also keeps a
detailed houseplant propagation log — she's currently rooting cuttings of a
Monstera adansonii, two pothos varieties, and an experimental ZZ plant.
Knows the difference between perlite and vermiculite and will explain it
unprompted. Reads slowly but deeply — currently in the middle of a Sandra
Cisneros short-story collection and revisiting *A Wrinkle in Time*.

Hobbies & media:
- Daily ballet, plus Pilates twice a week.
- Tends a window jungle of ~30 plants; runs a private Instagram for it.
- Watches Misty Copeland interviews, "Crash Course Botany" on YouTube,
  and "Anne with an E" on Netflix on repeat.
- Listens to film scores — Ludovico Einaudi, Joe Hisaishi — when working.

Learning style & teacher notes:
Meticulous note-taker — color-codes everything, redraws diagrams cleanly,
will rewrite a page if a single word is misspelled. Overstimulated by group
brainstorming and shuts down (visibly: shoulders rise, eye contact drops)
when called on cold. Will ask deep, sharp follow-up questions one-on-one
*after* the lesson, often the same day. Best move: hand her the question
ahead of time and let her think on it overnight; her contribution next class
is consistently the strongest. Sensitive to noise — let her sit by the
window. Connects abstract ideas to embodied movement easily; e.g. she'll
mime gradients as port de bras height changes if you let her. Avoid rushing
her transitions between tasks — she needs ~30 seconds of pause.
